page.title: Hedging and Cautious Language Basics
page.subtitle_html: Hedging and Cautious Language Basics - B2 cautious language for claims, opinions, and academic-style statements.
page.subtitle_text: B2 cautious language for claims, opinions, and academic-style statements.

blocks.body: {"level":"B2","intro":"Hedging and Cautious Language Basics: compact B2 anchors for making statements less absolute and more careful.","rules":[{"label":"Cautious verbs","text":"Use seem, appear, tend to, and suggest to soften claims.","example":"The results seem to support the idea."},{"label":"Cautious adverbs","text":"Use probably, possibly, generally, or relatively when the claim is not absolute.","example":"This approach is generally more effective."},{"label":"Careful conclusions","text":"Use may, might, and could to express possible conclusions.","example":"This change may reduce the risk."}]}

blocks.heading: B2 focus
blocks.body: This compact page exists as the theory anchor for the Polyglot English B2 lesson package.
